examples: Being learned in some branch of human knowledge is one thing; living in "public and illustrious thoughts," as Emerson would say, is something else.
The composer was one of many illustrious visitors to the town.
No, he would be a soldier, and return after long years, all war-worn and illustrious.
This first-rate museum, designed by illustrious architect IM Pei, is the crown jewel of Portland's thriving art scene.
Given Kidd's illustrious career, though, those end-of-career failures will amount to nothing more than footnotes.
